steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the "overall structure" of the text — meaning what the text does first and what it does second, not what it argues or evaluates.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It explores Dolly's lifelong interest in folk music, then debates whether pop music influenced "Jolene.": The passage never debates pop's influence on "Jolene" — it says folk ballads influenced that song. ✗
  • Choice B — It discusses a particular period in Dolly's recording career, then considers the music in an earlier period of her career.: The text opens on the late 1970s – early 1980s period ("Here You Come Again"), then the phrase "A decade earlier, however" pivots to her earlier folk roots ("Jolene"). ✓
  • Choice C — It enthusiastically recommends Dolly's earliest recordings, then expresses disappointment with her later recordings.: The text is descriptive, not evaluative. No recommendation or disappointment appears. ✗
  • Choice D — It praises "Here You Come Again," then notes how remarkably similar that song is to a song that Dolly recorded earlier in her career.: The text never compares the two songs to each other. ✗
That matches Choice B — It discusses a particular period in Dolly's recording career, then considers the music in an earlier period of her career..
